questions.question: Who is eligible to initially connect Gmail to Notion AI?

options.text: A Google admin and Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: Both a Google admin and the Notion workspace owner are required to establish the connection.

options.text: Any Notion user in the workspace
options.explanation: Regular Notion users cannot initiate the connection; admin rights are required.
A Notion admin alone is insufficient; a Google admin is also needed.

options.text: Any Gmail user with a personal account
options.explanation: Personal Gmail accounts are not eligible unless they use Notion Mail instead.

questions.question: What Notion plan must a workspace be on to use the Gmail AI Connector?

options.explanation: The free plan does not include access to AI Connectors.
Personal plans lack the Business/Enterprise features required for this integration.
Only Business or Enterprise plans provide the necessary permissions for the Gmail connector.
Team plans are not mentioned as eligible for the Gmail AI Connector.

questions.question: If a user does not have a paid Google Workspace plan, which alternative can they use to connect email to Notion AI?

options.explanation: Users without a paid Google Workspace plan can connect via Notion Mail instead of Gmail.
There is no separate "basic" integration for non‑paid Google accounts.
Free Gmail accounts cannot be linked directly; they must use Notion Mail.
An alternative (Notion Mail) does exist for non‑paid plans.

questions.question: How long after a new email arrives does Notion AI make it searchable?

options.explanation: Searchability is not truly instant; there is a short processing window.
New emails become searchable within a window of zero to thirty minutes after arrival.
The delay is much shorter than one to two hours.
Data is deleted after 24 hours upon disconnection, not delayed for search.

questions.question: Which statement best describes how Notion stores or references Gmail data?

options.text: Gmail data is permanently stored in Turbopuffer vector databases
options.explanation: The article explicitly says Notion does NOT store Gmail data in Turbopuffer.

options.text: Email content may be temporarily cached (e.g., via memcached) for faster search
options.explanation: Notion may temporarily cache email content to improve search speed, but does not keep it long‑term.

options.text: All Gmail messages are saved indefinitely on Notion servers
options.explanation: Notion follows data minimization; it does not keep messages indefinitely.

options.text: Notion never accesses Gmail data at any point
options.explanation: Notion AI reads Gmail messages to provide search functionality.

questions.question: According to Notion’s cross‑user security model, who can view another user’s email content in search results?

options.text: All members of the Notion workspace
options.explanation: Cross‑user security prevents any workspace member from seeing another’s emails.
Admins also cannot see other users’ emails; the restriction applies to everyone.

options.text: No one; each user only searches their own Gmail inbox
options.explanation: Notion AI enforces strict cross‑user security, limiting searches to the individual’s inbox.
Even the Google admin’s Gmail data is scoped to their own account when using Notion AI.

questions.question: Can a single Google Workspace be connected to multiple Notion workspaces?

options.text: Yes, you can connect as many Notion workspaces as you like
options.explanation: The integration is limited to a one‑to‑one relationship.

options.text: Yes, but only up to five Notion workspaces
options.explanation: There is no tiered limit; the rule is strictly one‑to‑one.

options.text: No, a Google Workspace can only be linked to a single Notion workspace and vice versa
options.explanation: The article states that the connection is one‑to‑one on both sides.

options.text: Yes, but only for paid Notion plans
options.explanation: Plan level does not change the one‑to‑one restriction.

questions.question: What is the correct navigation sequence for a workspace owner to disconnect Gmail from Notion AI?

options.text: Settings → Notion AI → Gmail gear icon → Disconnect
options.explanation: This is the exact path described for disconnecting the Gmail connector.
Integrations is not the section used for the Gmail AI connector.
The gear icon within Gmail settings is required; this path is incomplete.
While the Google admin may also delete the Marketplace app, the Notion UI path is as in option A.

questions.question: Can Notion AI read email attachments at this time?

options.explanation: Attachments are explicitly not read by Notion AI.

options.text: No, Notion AI cannot read attachments
options.explanation: The article clearly states that attachments are not read.
No attachment type is currently supported.
Images are also not processed by Notion AI.

questions.question: After a user disconnects Gmail from Notion AI, when is the associated email data deleted from Notion’s systems?

options.text: Immediately, as soon as the disconnect request is made
options.explanation: Data becomes unsearchable immediately, but actual deletion occurs within a short window.

options.text: Within 24 hours of the disconnection request
options.explanation: The article specifies that all associated data is deleted within 24 hours after disconnect.
The retention period is far shorter than a week.
Notion follows data‑minimization principles and deletes the data after disconnection.
